title: AutoHeal - User Input Protection: Focus Theft Detection & Auto-Recovery
desc: Multi-layer system protecting user focus from CDP/Chrome theft during ask operations. Layers: (1) prevFgHint early capture before EnsureCdpConnection, (2) SW_SHOWMINNOACTIVE(8) not SW_MINIMIZE(6) to avoid activating next window, (3) ForceForegroundWindow(AttachThreadInput) replacing silent-fail SetForegroundWindowRaw, (4) StartFocusWatchdog 200ms background loop during entire ask op, (5) RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync retry loop (150ms / 5s timeout) per step, (6) FocuslessWarningOverlay + ActionApi.OnFocusStealer prop stamp. Root cause gaps: AutoHeal never fires for CDP theft (side-effect not failure); SetForegroundWindowRaw blocked by Windows foreground lock; prevFg stale when captured after tab switch; no retry on restore failure.
steps: 1. prevFgGemini = GetForegroundWindow() BEFORE EnsureCdpConnection (not after tab switch)
2. EnsureCdpReadyAsync(prevFgHint) -- passes hint so EnsureCdp uses correct pre-switch prevFg
3. StartFocusWatchdog(prevFg, cdp) -- 200ms poll, Chrome-PID filter, ForceForegroundWindow per tick
4. GuardCdpFocusTheft after each insert step -- Task.Run RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync fire-and-forget
5. LogRestoreFocus at send/complete checkpoints -- delegates to RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync
6. RestoreFocusWithRetryAsync: snapshot IME+cursor, retry loop until restored, restore IME+cursor on success
8. ActionApi.OnFocusStealer stamp on Chrome + FocuslessWarningOverlay notification without focus steal
9. FocusStealer-ask prop in EnsureCdpReadyAsync triggers yield popup on next run
author: claude
